content.full_text:
25, 1977 
Ms. Valerie T. Smullen 
128 Kellogg Way 
Santa Clara, California 
95051 
Dear Ms. Smullen: 
Thank you for your letter of May 9 in which you asked whether 
a registered partisan political comntittee which makes a donation 
to a non-partisan city council candidate, is required to file 
campaign statements on the same basis as the candidate or would 
off-year statement fulfill the necessary filing obligation 
under the Political Reform Act. 
which contributes $250 or more must file under 
required by Section 84200 the same as the candidate. 
lation effective January I, 1977, however, permits 
supporting or opposing candidates in a local election 
to file an abbreviated form (Form 480) if it meets 
require-
ments 
on 
5 of the enclosed fact sheet titled "Reporting 
Guidelines for Campaign Committees." 
If no further contributions 
are received and no further expenditures are made or anticipated 
in connection with the election, the committee may designate the 
statement a ftfinal" statement by completing and attaching a Form 400 
thereby relieving itself of any further pre-election or post-
election filing. 
This does not, however, relieve the committee of 
its obligation to file an off-year (semi-annual) statement. 
A committee supporting or opposing a candidate in a local election 
and not contributing $250 or more need not file in connection with 
that election, but must file the required off-year statement. 
I 
enclosed some additional 
assistance to you 
of further ass 
Sincerely, 
mak 
Jeanne P. 
that will 
let me

sections.question: whether a registered partisan political committee which makes a donation to a non-partisan city council candidate, is required to file campaign statements on the same basis as the candidate or would off-year statement fulfill the necessary filing obligation under the Political Reform Act
sections.conclusion: A committee which contributes $250 or more must file under the requirements of Section 84200 the same as the candidate. Regulation effective January 1, 1977 permits committees supporting or opposing candidates in a local election to file an abbreviated form (Form 480). If no further contributions or expenditures are anticipated, the committee may designate a final statement (Form 400), relieving itself of pre-election and post-election filing obligations, though off-year filing remains required. A committee contributing less than $250 need not file in connection with that election but must file the required off-year statement.
sections.question_synthetic: Does a partisan political committee that donates to a non-partisan city council candidate need to file campaign statements on the same schedule as the candidate, or are off-year statements sufficient?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Committees contributing $250 or more must file on the same basis as candidates but may use an abbreviated Form 480 and can file a final statement to end pre- and post-election filing obligations; however, off-year statements are always required. Committees contributing less than $250 are only required to file off-year statements.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document dated May 25, 1977. Some text appears corrupted or partially illegible at the end ('mak Jeanne P.' and incomplete final paragraph). The conclusion section is reconstructed from fragmented text discussing filing requirements. No clear section headers marked as 'CONCLUSION' or 'QUESTION' but content clearly answers the inquiry in the opening paragraph.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: whether a registered partisan political committee which makes a donation to a non-partisan city council candidate, is required to file campaign statements on the same basis as the candidate or would off-year statement fulfill the necessary filing obligation under the Political Reform Act

CONCLUSION: A committee which contributes $250 or more must file under the requirements of Section 84200 the same as the candidate. Regulation effective January 1, 1977 permits committees supporting or opposing candidates in a local election to file an abbreviated form (Form 480). If no further contributions or expenditures are anticipated, the committee may designate a final statement (Form 400), relieving itself of pre-election and post-election filing obligations, though off-year filing remains required. A committee contributing less than $250 need not file in connection with that election but must file the required off-year statement.

If no further contributions are received and no further expenditures are made or anticipated in connection with the election, the committee may designate the statement a ftfinal" statement by completing and attaching a Form 400 thereby relieving itself of any further pre-election or post- election filing. embedding.summary: FPPC advises Ms. Smullen that a partisan political committee donating to a non-partisan city council candidate must file campaign statements based on contribution amount, with options for abbreviated forms and final statements, while maintaining off-year filing obligations.
